title: Representations of a Planar Graph
description: A single graph shown in three different ways: as an abstract graph, a planar embedding (straight-line drawing), and its dual graph representation.
relationships: The Abstract Graph is the underlying structure
The Planar Embedding is one possible geometric realization of the abstract graph on a plane without edge crossings
The Dual Graph (G*) is constructed from the planar embedding by representing faces as vertices and adjacent faces as connected edges
